Making the Client Library accessible to clients introduces complexity because bugs in the library become bugs in the application binary. However, direct client access to storage nodes is vastly more network- and hardware resource efficient than a proxy design, avoiding an extra network hop for terabytes of data per second.

Unfortunately, direct storage node access is a poor fit for remote requests, where the client is geographically distant from the Tectonic cluster. The additional network overhead makes the orchestration round trips prohibitively inefficient. To solve this problem, Tectonic handles remote data access differently from local data access: remote requests get forwarded to a stateless proxy in the same datacenter as the storage nodes.
sections.translationMarkdown:
Tectonic 的设计通常优先考虑简洁性而非效率。下面讨论两个为了获得性能收益而选择引入额外复杂性的例子。

**管理 reconstruction 负载。** RS 编码的数据可以采用连续方式存储，即将一个 data block 划分为多个 chunk，并将每个 chunk 连续写入 storage node；也可以采用条带方式存储，即将一个 data block 划分为小得多的 chunk，再以 round-robin 方式将它们分布到各 storage node [51]。由于 Tectonic 使用连续 RS 编码，且大多数读取都小于一个 chunk，因此读取通常是直接读取：它们无须执行 RS reconstruction，所以只包含一次磁盘 IO。Reconstruction read 所需的 IO 数量是直接读取的 10 倍（对于 RS(10,4) 编码）。尽管 reconstruction read 很常见，但很难预测需要进行 reconstruction 的读取比例，因为硬件故障和节点过载都会触发 reconstruction。我们的经验表明，如果不加控制，这种资源需求的大幅变化可能会造成级联故障，影响系统可用性与性能。

如果某些 storage node 过载，直接读取就会失败并触发 reconstruction read。这会增加系统其余部分的负载，进而触发更多 reconstruction read，如此循环。Reconstruction 的这种级联被称为 reconstruction storm。一种简单的解决方案是使用条带化 RS 编码，使所有读取都执行 reconstruction。由于发生故障时读取所需的 IO 数量不会改变，因此这可以避免 reconstruction storm。但它会让正常情况下的读取昂贵得多。我们的做法是将 reconstruction read 限制为全部读取的 10%，以防止 reconstruction storm。在我们的生产集群中，这一比例的 reconstruction read 通常足以处理磁盘、主机和机架故障。通过承担一定的调优复杂性，我们避免了磁盘资源的过量配置。

**高效访问数据中心内部及跨数据中心的数据。** Tectonic 允许客户端直接访问 storage node；另一种设计则可以使用前端 proxy 来中介客户端对存储的全部访问。让客户端能够访问 Client Library 会引入复杂性，因为 library 中的 bug 会变成应用程序二进制文件中的 bug。然而，与 proxy 设计相比，客户端直接访问 storage node 的网络和硬件资源效率要高得多，能够避免每秒数 TB 数据所需的一次额外网络跳转。

遗憾的是，直接访问 storage node 并不适合远程请求，因为此时客户端在地理位置上远离 Tectonic 集群。额外的网络开销会使编排所需的往返低效到不可接受。为了解决这个问题，Tectonic 对远程数据访问和本地数据访问采用不同的处理方式：远程请求会被转发到一个与 storage node 位于同一数据中心的无状态 proxy。

sections.title: 6.5 Tradeoffs and Compromises
sections.originalMarkdown:
Migrating to Tectonic was not without tradeoffs and compromises. This subsection describes a few areas where Tectonic is either less flexible or less performant than Facebook’s previous infrastructure. We also describe the impact of using a hash-partitioned metadata store.

**The impact of higher metadata latency.** Migrating to Tectonic meant data warehouse applications saw higher metadata latency. HDFS metadata operations are in-memory and all metadata for a namespace is stored on a single node. In contrast, Tectonic stores its metadata in a sharded key-value store instance and disaggregates metadata layers (§3.3). This means Tectonic metadata operations may require one or more network calls (e.g., a file open operation will interact with the Name and File layers). Data warehouse had to adjust how it handled certain metadata operations given the additional metadata latency. For instance, compute engines rename a set of files one by one, in sequence, after computation is done. In HDFS each rename was fast, but with Tectonic, compute engines parallelize this step to hide the extra latency of individual Tectonic rename operations.

**Working around hash-partitioned metadata.** Because Tectonic directories are hash sharded, listing directories recursively involves querying many shards. In fact, Tectonic does not provide a recursive list API; tenants need to build it as a client-side wrapper over individual list calls. As a result, unlike HDFS, Tectonic does not have du (directory utilization) functionality to query aggregate space usage of a directory. Instead, Tectonic periodically aggregates per-directory usage statistics, which can be stale.
sections.translationMarkdown:
迁移到 Tectonic 并非没有权衡与折中。本小节介绍了 Tectonic 相较于 Facebook 先前基础设施灵活性较差或性能较低的几个方面。我们还会说明使用 hash-partitioned metadata store 所带来的影响。

**较高 metadata 延迟的影响。** 迁移到 Tectonic 意味着 数据仓库 应用程序会遇到更高的 metadata 延迟。HDFS 的 metadata 操作在内存中完成，并且一个 namespace 的全部 metadata 都存储在单个节点上。相比之下，Tectonic 将 metadata 存储在一个分片的 key-value store 实例中，并将各 metadata 层解耦（§3.3）。这意味着 Tectonic 的 metadata 操作可能需要一次或多次网络调用（例如，一次文件打开操作会与 Name 层和 File 层交互）。考虑到额外的 metadata 延迟，数据仓库 必须调整某些 metadata 操作的处理方式。例如，计算完成后，计算引擎会依次逐个重命名一组文件。在 HDFS 中，每次重命名都很快；而使用 Tectonic 时，计算引擎会将该步骤并行化，以隐藏各次 Tectonic 重命名操作带来的额外延迟。

**绕开 hash-partitioned metadata 的限制。** 由于 Tectonic 目录采用 hash sharding，递归列出目录需要查询许多 shard。实际上，Tectonic 不提供递归 list API；租户需要在客户端将多次独立的 list 调用封装起来，自行构建这一功能。因此，与 HDFS 不同，Tectonic 没有用于查询目录聚合空间使用量的 du（directory utilization）功能。作为替代，Tectonic 会定期聚合每个目录的使用情况统计信息，而这些信息可能不是最新的。

sections.title: 6.6 Design and Deployment Lessons
sections.originalMarkdown:
**Achieving high scalability is an iterative process enabled by a microservice architecture.** Several Tectonic components have been through multiple iterations to meet increasing scalability requirements. For example, the first version of the Chunk Store grouped blocks to reduce metadata. A number of blocks with the same redundancy scheme were grouped and RS-encoded as one unit to store their chunks together. Each block group mapped to a set of storage nodes. This is a common technique since it significantly reduces metadata [37, 53], but it was too inflexible for our production environment. For example, with only 5% of storage nodes unavailable, 80% of the block groups became unavailable for writes. This design also precluded optimizations like hedged quorum writes and quorum appends (§5).

Additionally, our initial Metadata Store architecture did not separate the Name and File layers; clients consulted the same shards for directory lookups and for listing blocks in a file. This design resulted in unavailability from metadata hotspots, prompting us to further disaggregate metadata.

Tectonic’s evolution shows the importance of trying new designs to get closer to performance goals. Our development experience also shows the value of a microservices-based architecture for experimentation: we could iterate on components transparently to the rest of the system.

**Memory corruption is common at scale.** At Tectonic’s scale, with thousands of machines reading and writing a large amount of data every day, in-memory data corruption is a regular occurrence, a phenomenon observed in other large-scale systems [12, 27]. We address this by enforcing checksum checks within and between process boundaries.

For data $D$ and checksum $C_D$, if we want to perform an in-memory transformation $F$ such that $D' = F(D)$, we generate checksum $C_{D'}$ for $D'$. To check $D'$, we must convert $D'$ back to $D$ with $G$, the inverse function of $F$, and compare $C_{G(D')}$ with $C_D$. The inverse function, $G$, may be expensive to compute (e.g., for RS encoding or encryption), but it is an acceptable cost for Tectonic to preserve data integrity.

All API boundaries involving moving, copying, or transforming data had to be retrofitted to include checksum information. Clients pass a checksum with data to the Client Library when writing, and Tectonic needs to pass the checksum not just across process boundaries (e.g., between the client library and the storage node) but also within the process (e.g., after transformations). Checking the integrity of transformations prevents corruptions from propagating to reconstructed chunks after storage node failure.
sections.translationMarkdown:
**实现高可扩展性是一个由微服务架构所支持的迭代过程。** 为满足不断增长的可扩展性需求，Tectonic 的多个组件都经历了多次迭代。例如，第一版 Chunk Store 将 block 分组，以减少 metadata。它将多个采用相同冗余方案的 block 组合起来，作为一个整体进行 RS 编码，并将它们的 chunk 存储在一起。每个 block group 映射到一组 storage node。这是一种常见技术，因为它能够显著减少 metadata [37, 53]，但对于我们的生产环境来说过于缺乏灵活性。例如，仅有 5% 的 storage node 不可用时，80% 的 block group 就会变得不可写。该设计还排除了 hedged quorum write 和 quorum append（§5）之类的优化。

此外，最初的 Metadata Store 架构并未分离 Name 层与 File 层；客户端在执行目录查找和列出文件中的 block 时会查询相同的 shard。这种设计因 metadata hotspot 导致系统不可用，促使我们进一步解耦 metadata。

Tectonic 的演进表明，为了更加接近性能目标，尝试新设计非常重要。我们的开发经验还体现了基于微服务的架构对实验的价值：我们可以对组件进行迭代，而对系统其他部分保持透明。

**在大规模环境中，内存数据损坏很常见。** 在 Tectonic 的规模下，数千台机器每天都要读写大量数据，内存数据损坏是一种经常发生的现象；其他大规模系统中也观察到了这一现象 [12, 27]。我们通过在进程边界内部以及跨进程边界强制执行 checksum 检查来解决该问题。

对于数据 $D$ 及其 checksum $C_D$，如果要执行内存变换 $F$，使得 $D' = F(D)$，我们会为 $D'$ 生成 checksum $C_{D'}$。为了检查 $D'$，必须使用 $F$ 的逆函数 $G$ 将 $D'$ 转换回 $D$，并将 $C_{G(D')}$ 与 $C_D$ 进行比较。逆函数 $G$ 的计算成本可能很高（例如 RS 编码或加密的逆函数），但为了保护数据完整性，对 Tectonic 而言这是可以接受的代价。

所有涉及移动、复制或变换数据的 API 边界都必须经过改造，以包含 checksum 信息。客户端在写入时将 checksum 与数据一同传给 Client Library，而 Tectonic 不仅需要跨进程边界传递 checksum（例如，在 client library 与 storage node 之间），还需要在进程内部传递 checksum（例如，在变换之后）。检查变换的完整性，可以防止 storage node 故障后，数据损坏传播到 reconstruction 得到的 chunk。

sections.title: 6.7 Services that do not use Tectonic
sections.titleTranslation: 6.7 不使用 Tectonic 的服务
sections.originalMarkdown:
Some services within Facebook do not use Tectonic for storage. Bootstrap services, e.g., the software binary package deployment system, which must have no dependencies, cannot use Tectonic because it depends on many other services (e.g., the key-value store, configuration management system, deployment management system). Graph storage [16] also does not use Tectonic, as Tectonic is not yet optimized for key-value store workloads which often need the low latencies provided by SSD storage.

Many other services do not use Tectonic directly. They instead use Tectonic through a major tenant like blob storage or data warehouse. This is because a core design philosophy of Tectonic is separation of concerns. Internally, Tectonic aims for independent software layers which each focus on a narrow set of a storage system’s core responsibilities (e.g., storage nodes only know about chunks but not blocks or files). This philosophy extends to how Tectonic fits in with the rest of the storage infrastructure. For example, Tectonic focuses on providing fault tolerance within a datacenter; it does not protect against datacenter failures. Geo-replication is a separate problem that Tectonic delegates to its large tenants, who solve it to provide transparent and easy-to-use shared storage for applications. Tenants are also expected to know details of capacity management and storage deployments and rebalancing across different datacenters. For smaller applications, the complexity and implementation needed to interface directly with Tectonic in a way that meets their storage needs would amount to re-implementing features that tenants have already implemented. Individual applications therefore use Tectonic via tenants.
sections.translationMarkdown:
Facebook 内部的一些服务不使用 Tectonic 进行存储。Bootstrap service（例如软件二进制包部署系统）不得具有任何依赖，因此无法使用 Tectonic，因为 Tectonic 依赖许多其他服务（例如 key-value store、配置管理系统和部署管理系统）。Graph storage [16] 也不使用 Tectonic，因为 Tectonic 尚未针对 key-value store 工作负载进行优化，而这类工作负载通常需要 SSD 存储所提供的低延迟。

还有许多其他服务并不直接使用 Tectonic，而是通过 blob storage 或 数据仓库 这样的主要租户来使用 Tectonic。这是因为 Tectonic 的核心设计理念之一是关注点分离。在内部，Tectonic 力求让各软件层彼此独立，并让每一层只关注存储系统的一小部分核心职责（例如，storage node 只了解 chunk，而不了解 block 或文件）。这一理念也延伸到了 Tectonic 与其余存储基础设施的集成方式。例如，Tectonic 专注于在单个数据中心内部提供容错能力，而不防范数据中心故障。Geo-replication 是一个独立问题，Tectonic 将它交给大型租户解决；大型租户通过解决该问题，为应用程序提供透明且易用的共享存储。租户还应当了解容量管理、存储部署以及跨不同数据中心重新平衡的细节。对于较小的应用程序而言，若要以满足自身存储需求的方式直接与 Tectonic 对接，所需的复杂性和实现工作相当于重新实现租户已经实现的功能。因此，各个应用程序会通过租户使用 Tectonic。

sections.title: 7 Related Work
sections.originalMarkdown:
Tectonic adapts techniques from existing systems and the literature, demonstrating how they can be combined into a novel system that realizes exabyte-scale single clusters which support a diversity of workloads on a shared storage fabric.

**Distributed filesystems with a single metadata node.** HDFS [15], GFS [24], and others [38, 40, 44] are limited by the metadata node to tens of petabytes of storage per instance or cluster, compared to Tectonic’s exabytes per cluster.

**Federating namespaces for increased capacity.** Federated HDFS [8] and Windows Azure Storage (WAS) [17] combine multiple smaller storage clusters (with a single metadata node) into larger clusters. For instance, a federated HDFS [8] cluster has multiple independent single-namenode namespaces, even though the storage nodes are shared between namespaces. Federated systems still have the operational complexity of bin-packing datasets (§2). Also, migrating or sharing data between instances, e.g., to load-balance or add storage capacity, requires resource-heavy data copying among namespaces [33, 46, 54]

**Hash-based data location for metadata scalability.** Ceph [53] and FDS [36] eliminate centralized metadata, instead locating data by hashing on object ID. Handling failures in such systems is a scalability bottleneck. Failures are more frequent with larger clusters, requiring frequent updates to the hash-to-location map that must propagate to all nodes. Yahoo’s Cloud Object Store [41] federates Ceph instances to isolate the effects of failures. Furthermore, adding hardware and draining is complicated, as Ceph lacks support for controlled data migration [52]. Tectonic explicitly maps chunks to storage nodes, allowing controlled migration.

**Disaggregated or sharded metadata for scalability.** Like Tectonic, ADLS [42] and HopsFS [35] increase filesystem capacity by disaggregating metadata into layers in separate sharded data stores. Tectonic hash-partitions directories, while ADLS and HopsFS store some related directory metadata on the same shards, causing metadata for related parts of the directory tree to be colocated. Hash partitioning helps Tectonic avoid hotspots local to part of the directory tree. ADLS uses WAS’s federated architecture [17] for block storage. In contrast, Tectonic’s block storage is flat.

Like Tectonic, Colossus [28, 32] provides cluster-wide multi-exabyte storage where client libraries directly access storage nodes. Colossus uses Spanner [21], a globally consistent database to store filesystem metadata. Tectonic metadata is built on a sharded key-value store, which only provides within-shard strong consistency and no cross-shard operations. These limitations have not been a problem in practice.

**Blob and object stores.** Compared to distributed filesystems, blob and object stores [14, 18, 36, 37] are easier to scale, as they do not have a hierarchical directory tree or namespace to keep consistent. Hierarchical namespaces are required for most warehouse workloads.

**Other large-scale storage systems.** Lustre [1] and GPFS [45] are tuned for high-throughput parallel access. Lustre limits the number of metadata nodes, limiting scalability. GPFS is POSIX-compliant, introducing unnecessary metadata management overhead for our setting. HBase [9] is a key-value store based on HDFS, but its HDFS clusters are not shared with a warehouse workload. We could not compare with AWS [2] as its design is not public.

**Multitenancy techniques.** Tectonic’s multitenancy techniques were co-designed with the filesystem as well as the tenants, and does not aim to achieve optimal fair sharing. It is thus easier to provide performance isolation compared to other systems in the literature. Other systems use more complex resource management techniques to accommodate changes in tenancy and resource use policies, or to provide optimal fair resource sharing among tenants [25, 48, 49].

Some details of Tectonic have previously been described in talks [39, 47] where the system is called Warm Storage.
sections.translationMarkdown:
Tectonic 对现有系统和文献中的技术进行了调整，并展示了如何将这些技术组合成一个新颖的系统：该系统能够实现 EB 级单集群，并在共享存储基础设施上支持多种工作负载。

**采用单个 metadata node 的分布式文件系统。** HDFS [15]、GFS [24] 及其他系统 [38, 40, 44] 受 metadata node 限制，每个实例或集群只能提供数十 PB 存储；相比之下，Tectonic 的每个集群可达到 EB 级。

**通过 namespace federation 提升容量。** Federated HDFS [8] 和 Windows Azure Storage（WAS）[17] 将多个较小的存储集群（各自采用单个 metadata node）组合成更大的集群。例如，一个 federated HDFS [8] 集群拥有多个相互独立的 single-namenode namespace，尽管这些 namespace 共享 storage node。Federated 系统仍然存在对数据集进行 bin-packing 的运维复杂性（§2）。此外，在实例之间迁移或共享数据，例如为了负载均衡或增加存储容量，需要在 namespace 之间执行消耗大量资源的数据复制 [33, 46, 54]

**基于 hash 的数据定位，以实现 metadata 可扩展性。** Ceph [53] 和 FDS [36] 消除了集中式 metadata，转而通过对 object ID 进行 hash 来定位数据。在这类系统中，故障处理是可扩展性的瓶颈。集群越大，故障越频繁，因此必须频繁更新 hash-to-location map，并将更新传播到所有节点。Yahoo 的 Cloud Object Store [41] 将多个 Ceph 实例联合起来，以隔离故障影响。此外，添加硬件和排空节点也很复杂，因为 Ceph 不支持受控数据迁移 [52]。Tectonic 将 chunk 显式映射到 storage node，从而支持受控迁移。

**通过解耦或分片 metadata 实现可扩展性。** 与 Tectonic 类似，ADLS [42] 和 HopsFS [35] 将 metadata 解耦成多个层，并把这些层放入彼此独立的分片式 data store 中，以提高文件系统容量。Tectonic 对目录进行 hash partitioning，而 ADLS 和 HopsFS 则将部分相关目录 metadata 存储在相同 shard 上，使目录树相关部分的 metadata 被 colocate。Hash partitioning 帮助 Tectonic 避免目录树局部区域的 hotspot。ADLS 使用 WAS 的 federated architecture [17] 进行 block 存储。相比之下，Tectonic 的 block 存储是扁平的。

与 Tectonic 类似，Colossus [28, 32] 提供集群范围的多 EB 存储，其中 client library 直接访问 storage node。Colossus 使用 Spanner [21]——一个全局一致的数据库——来存储文件系统 metadata。Tectonic 的 metadata 构建在分片式 key-value store 之上，该 store 只提供 shard 内强一致性，不提供跨 shard 操作。这些限制在实践中并未造成问题。

**Blob store 和 object store。** 与分布式文件系统相比，blob store 和 object store [14, 18, 36, 37] 更容易扩展，因为它们没有需要保持一致的分层目录树或 namespace。大多数 warehouse 工作负载都需要分层 namespace。

**其他大规模存储系统。** Lustre [1] 和 GPFS [45] 针对高吞吐并行访问进行了调优。Lustre 限制 metadata node 的数量，进而限制了可扩展性。GPFS 遵循 POSIX，给我们的使用场景引入了不必要的 metadata 管理开销。HBase [9] 是基于 HDFS 的 key-value store，但它的 HDFS 集群不会与 warehouse 工作负载共享。由于 AWS [2] 的设计并未公开，我们无法与之比较。

**多租户技术。** Tectonic 的多租户技术与文件系统和租户协同设计，并不以实现最优公平共享为目标。因此，与文献中的其他系统相比，Tectonic 更容易提供性能隔离。其他系统使用更复杂的资源管理技术，以适应租户构成和资源使用策略的变化，或者在租户之间提供最优公平资源共享 [25, 48, 49]。

Tectonic 的一些细节此前已在演讲 [39, 47] 中介绍过，当时该系统被称为 Warm Storage。

sections.title: 8 Conclusion
sections.originalMarkdown: This paper presents Tectonic, Facebook’s distributed filesystem. A single Tectonic instance can support all Facebook’s major storage tenants in a datacenter, enabling better resource utilization and less operational complexity. Tectonic’s hash-sharded disaggregated metadata and flat data chunk storage allow it to address and store exabytes. Its cardinality-reduced resource management allows it to efficiently and fairly share resources and distribute surplus resources for high utilization. Tectonic’s client-driven tenant-specific optimizations allow it to match or exceed the performance of the previous specialized storage systems.
sections.translationMarkdown: 本文介绍了 Facebook 的分布式文件系统 Tectonic。单个 Tectonic 实例可以支持 Facebook 在一个数据中心内的所有主要存储租户，从而实现更高的资源利用率和更低的运维复杂性。Tectonic 采用 hash-sharded、解耦的 metadata 以及扁平的 data chunk 存储，因此能够寻址并存储 EB 级数据。它通过降低资源管理的基数，得以高效、公平地共享资源并分配富余资源，从而实现较高的利用率。Tectonic 由客户端驱动的租户特定优化，使其性能能够达到或超过之前的专用存储系统。
